item.title: Make Energy Affordable by Taxing Billionaires, Says UK’s Green Leader
item.description: Zack Polanski discusses energy, defence and whether he can keep on the right side of the UK bond market.

item.title: Africa’s Biggest City Takes Out $7.5 Million Flood-Risk Policy
item.description: Lagos, Nigeria’s commercial hub, has secured a $7.5 million flood insurance policy so some of its poorest residents can access relief when disaster strikes.

item.title: Chevron Says Australia LNG Output Offline After Cyclone
item.description: Liquefied natural gas production in Australia at two plants operated by Chevron Corp. has been reduced or shut after a tropical cyclone, according to a Chevron Australia spokesperson.

item.title: Arctic Sea Ice Ties Ominous Record Amid Geopolitical Upheaval
item.description: Wintertime ice shrank to a record-low level for the second year running, a sign of how climate change is reshaping the region.

item.title: The Solution to Soaring Gas Prices? For Some, It’s Going Electric
item.description: The Iran War’s impact on energy prices is spurring people around the world to abandon fossil fuels in favor of cheaper electric alternatives.

item.title: A Decade of Global Climate Caution Is Sealed by India’s Wary Goals
item.description: Meager new emissions reduction plans from New Delhi limit the prospects of major progress through 2035 from the three biggest polluting nations.

item.title: Iran War Prompts UK to Restart Factory Over Risk of CO2 Shortage
item.description: The UK government temporarily restarted an ethanol plant over concerns that the Iran war will cause a shortage of carbon dioxide, a byproduct used widely in food production and healthcare.

item.title: Energy Traders Turn to AI to Forecast the Weather Forecast
item.description: In Europe’s weather-driven energy markets, traders are turning to AI and machine-learning tools designed not to predict temperatures and precipitation, but to forecast the forecast.

item.title: FEMA to Offer $1 Billion Through Embattled Disaster Mitigation Fund
item.description: The move comes after a judge ordered the agency to restore the program, which it had cancelled last year.

item.title: Iran War Is Pushing Consumers to Break Up With Fossil Fuels
item.description: EVs, solar panels, induction stoves and heat pumps, now cheaper than ever, are becoming more attractive as the conflict upends oil and gas markets.

item.title: Cyclone Narelle Set to Lash Australian LNG, Iron Ore Ports
item.description: Tropical Cyclone Narelle is threatening to disrupt industrial operations along Australia’s northwestern coast, after leaving mine closures and evacuations in its wake along the north shore over the past week.

item.title: Mexico Bets on Supercomputer to Combat Extreme Weather Events
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum is working to develop climate models that will help prevent extreme weather events, part of a push to set up a public supercomputer that can use millions of data points to tackle national challenges.

item.title: Green Leader Polanski Says the UK Shouldn’t ‘Strangle’ Business
item.description: The leader of the UK’s insurgent Green Party said government shouldn’t be “strangling” business, as he tempered his left-wing bloc’s economic message ahead of nationwide local elections in May.

item.title: Alberta and Canada Reach Deal on Oil and Gas Methane Emissions
item.description: The Alberta and Canadian governments have reached a deal to reduce methane emissions, the latest in a series of energy negotiations setting the stage for a new pipeline to the Pacific coast.

item.title: This Is Where Global Warming Poses the Most Deadly Risks
item.description: New research models how many more deaths extreme heat could cause by 2050.

item.title: Growing Number of ESG-Focused Funds Are Liquidated in US
item.description: The market for ESG-focused funds keeps shrinking.

item.title: Singapore Delays Carbon Levy on Flights, Citing Middle East War
item.description: Singapore is delaying a sustainable aviation fuel levy that airline customers were meant to start paying next month, due to the surge in fuel costs spurred by the Iran war.

item.title: India’s Delayed Climate Plans Set Modest New Emissions Targets
item.description: India’s long-delayed new climate policy set a modest target to cut a key emissions metric 47% by 2035, as the world’s third-largest polluter attempts to balance growing energy demand and action on global warming.

item.title: Atlantic to See Up to 15 Storms This Year, AccuWeather Says
item.description: The Atlantic hurricane season is expected to produce 11 to 15 named storms, near the long-term average, with the highest risks along the northern and eastern portions of the US Gulf Coast and the Carolinas, AccuWeather Inc. said.

item.title: Philippine Pension Fund Offers $8,300 Solar Loans to Cut Energy Bills
item.description: Philippine state-owned pension fund Government Service Insurance System is offering loans of up to 500,000 pesos ($8,300) for members to buy and install solar power for their homes amid surging fossil fuel prices.

item.title: JPMorgan Executive Calls Out ‘Vague’ Contracts in Carbon Markets
item.description: An executive at JPMorgan Chase & Co. has warned that developers of carbon-credit projects may struggle to find banks to finance deals, unless the purchase contracts they enter live up to a number of basic standards.

item.title: Honda, Sony Drop Afeela EV Project, Reassess Partnership
item.description: Honda Motor Co. and Sony Group Corp. have scrapped plans to jointly develop an electric vehicle and will rethink their partnership as the carmaker’s financial troubles deepen.

item.title: High Levels of Cancer-Causing Benzene in European Cooking Gas, Study Finds
item.description: Domestic gas supply in several European cities contains high levels of benzene, an odorless substance that can cause cancer, according to research released on Wednesday.

item.title: Future Heat Danger Differs Starkly for Rich and Poor Countries
item.description: Lower-income nations will face 10 times as many heat-related deaths as high-income ones, researchers estimate.

item.title: Development Lender Aims to Speed Post-War Shift to Clean Energy
item.description: The Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank aims to help the region accelerate adoption of cleaner power as nations respond to energy shocks from the Iran war by seeking greater self-sufficiency.

item.title: Energy Shocks Will Slow Climate Action, Top Mining Executive Says
item.description: Ongoing energy disruptions will set back efforts to curb greenhouse gas emissions as nations prioritize supply security, according to one of the most senior executives at the world’s top mining company, BHP Group.

item.title: Prominent Researcher Exits NASA, Citing US Attacks on Science
item.description: Climate expert Kate Marvel pointed to “upheavals” under the Trump administration as a reason for her departure.

item.title: Battery Startup Form Energy Inks Data Center Deal With Crusoe
item.description: The deal to deliver batteries that can store and discharge 100 hours of power signals growing demand for long-duration storage.

item.title: Crusoe Buys Batteries That Last for Days to Power New Data Centers
item.description: The deal with Form Energy reflects growing interest in a niche technology that can help provide power when prices are high or when the grid is under stress.

item.title: JPMorgan Sees ‘National Security Risk’ in Old Grid Networks
item.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. says aging, run-down grid infrastructure now risks undermining security goals, with everything from extreme weather to cyberattacks posing a growing threat.
